strengths: Communication example with the fashion designer shows clear preparation and effective collaboration skills applied to your learning goal (B1).
Excellent use of OPVL and a KWL chart to organize research, demonstrating depth in information literacy and supporting your learning objectives (B1).
Interview summary table provides concrete evidence of how stakeholder communication influenced your product design, effectively showcasing ATL application to product development (B2).
Creative thinking narrative captures your brainstorming and ideation processes, reflecting thoughtful application of design skills (B2).
weaknesses: âWhat I knowâ entries in the KWL chart are too broad; include specific prior knowledge about cancer awareness to deepen connections to your projectâs design decisions.
Color theory choices for awareness apparel are explained but not linked to customer feedback or sales data; integrating these metrics would demonstrate their impact on product success.
Instagram post designs are visually engaging but lack engagement metrics (likes, shares, comments); including these data points would quantify how creative thinking affected audience reach (B2).
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your application of ATL skills is supported by strong examples in communication and information literacy, and your use of interview data and creative narratives illustrates thoughtful product development. To deepen this section, specify your prior knowledge in research charts, connect design decisions to feedback or sales data, and incorporate social media engagement metrics. These enhancements will provide the detailed evidence needed to demonstrate the full impact of your skill applications.

strengths: Clear and specific learning goal detailing design, marketing, and emotional support objectives, providing strong alignment with personal interest and guiding the project effectively.
Detailed timeline in Figure 3 that sequences tasks and dates, offering a clear roadmap and strengthening the feasibility of the plan.
weaknesses: Learning goal lacks measurable outcomes linked explicitly to personal experience; needs specific indicators to fully meet A1 requirements.
Product goal description omits a detailed sequence of activities and resource allocation; a comprehensive project timeline and resource list are needed for A2.
Success criteria table shows evidence of thought but the rationale entries are truncated; all âWhy this criterion is testedâ cells must be complete and clearly articulated.
Action plan remains at a high level; specific timelines, milestones, and resource assignments should be added to satisfy A3.
Expanded success criteria address function and cost but do not integrate budget and personnel considerations; linking resource planning directly to criteria would strengthen feasibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates strong alignment between your learning goal and personal interest, supported by a well-structured timeline. To move from a solid to an exemplary plan, refine your learning goal with measurable outcomes, flesh out the sequencing and resource allocation in your product plan, and complete the rationale in your success criteria. Adding concrete timelines, milestones, and a detailed resource list will ensure your plan fully meets the rubricâs highest standards.

strengths: Sales data table clearly presents production versus profit and underpins your evaluation of product function, supporting C2.
Audience interaction pie chart effectively illustrates repurchase behavior, providing robust evidence for your evaluation of audience success (C2).
Overall evaluation integrates multiple success criteria with reflective insight, demonstrating thorough product analysis (C2).
Reflection on time management and balancing pressure shows resilience and discipline, giving strong evidence of personal impact and learning growth (C1).
Leveraging positive customer feedback to evaluate product form strengthens your product analysis and shows responsiveness to stakeholder input (C2).
Reflection on combining art and advocacy highlights personal growth and aligns well with IB learner attributes (C1).
weaknesses: Analysis of underperforming sales tactics is limited; you should identify which marketing approaches fell short and propose concrete adjustments to improve future outcomes (C2).
Customer feedback is used effectively but lacks diversity; including a broader range of perspectives would illustrate inclusivity and deepen your product evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections are rich with data-driven insights and personal growth narratives, especially in evaluating sales and audience engagement. To elevate this section, critically analyze underperforming marketing tactics and outline actionable improvements. Additionally, incorporate diverse customer perspectives to ensure your evaluation reflects a comprehensive and inclusive understanding of stakeholder experiences.

annotations.comment: You note not meeting your sales target; analyze which marketing tactics underperformed and propose adjustments to improve product function in future projects (C2).
The sales data table clearly presents production versus profit and supports your evaluation of product function, enhancing your C2 analysis.
The audience interaction pie chart effectively illustrates repurchase behavior, providing robust evidence for your C2 evaluation of audience success.
Your overall evaluation integrates multiple criteria with reflective insight, demonstrating a thorough C2 analysis and clear understanding of project impact.
'What I know' entries in your KWL chart remain broad; include specific prior knowledge about cancer awareness to deepen connections to your projectâs design decisions (B1).
Your communication example with the fashion designer clearly shows preparation and clarity in exchanges, evidencing strong collaboration skills applied to your learning goal (B1).
Reflecting on time management and balance under pressure demonstrates resilience and discipline, providing solid evidence of personal impact and learning (C1).
Define your learning goal with specific, measurable outcomes that clearly link to your personal experience, strengthening the connection between interest and goal and meeting A1 requirements.
Leveraging positive customer feedback to evaluate product form strengthens your C2 evaluation; consider adding diverse customer perspectives to illustrate inclusivity.
Your learning goal is clear and specific, detailing design, marketing, and emotional support objectives, demonstrating strong alignment with A1 and guiding your project effectively.
The product goal outlines scope and targets but lacks a detailed sequence of activities and resource allocation; include a project timeline and resource list to fully meet A2.
The success criteria table is detailed for form but the 'Why this criterion is tested' cell appears truncated; ensure all rationale entries are complete and clearly articulated for A2.
The action plan describes its importance but remains high-level; add specific timelines, milestones, and resource assignments to fully satisfy A3 requirements.
Your expanded success criteria cover function and cost but could better link resource planning; integrate budget and personnel considerations to strengthen A2 feasibility.
The detailed timeline in Fig. 3 effectively sequences tasks and dates, providing a clear roadmap that strengthens your A3 plan.
Excellent use of OPVL and a KWL chart to organize research demonstrates depth in applying information literacy, effectively supporting your learning goal (B1).
The interview summary table provides concrete evidence of how communication influenced your product design, effectively showcasing ATL application to product development (B2).
Good understanding of color theory for awareness apparel; strengthen by linking these choices to customer feedback or sales data to demonstrate their impact on product success (B2).
Your creative thinking narrative captures brainstorming and ideation; include specific instances where these processes led to key design refinements to show depth in B2 application.
The Instagram post designs are visually engaging; incorporate engagement metrics (likes, shares, comments) to quantify how creative thinking affected audience reach and impact (B2).
Your reflection on combining art and advocacy shows clear personal growth and resonance with IB learner attributes, effectively addressing C1.
